items.title: U.S. CISA adds a Cisco IOS flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
items.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a Cisco IOS flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)added a Cisco IOS flaw, tracked as CVE-2008-4128, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. Cisco IOS 12.4 running on Cisco 871 Integrated Services Routers contains multiple CSRF flaws in []

items.kev_name: Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability
items.kev_action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

title: SENTINEL APEX — US CISA adds a Cisco IOS flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
id: a00b5e47-b21c-575c-a76f-29e6a8b770aa
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2008-4128 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2008-4128
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/13
modified: 2026/07/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2008-4128 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-13T21:14:10Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2008-4128", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2008-4128 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1291420; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: GeoNode: Stored XSS to full account takeover
items.description: An issue exists within GEONODE where the current rich text editor is vulnerable to Stored XSS. The applications cookies are set securely, but it is possible to retrieve a victims CSRF token and issue a request to change another user's email address to perform a full account takeover. Due to the script element not impacting the CORS policy, requests will succeed.
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] OSS-ADVISORY advisory. Risk score 6.1/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P2] Cross-Site Scripting - moderate severity (Risk 6.1/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. AI confidence: LOW (20%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

items.title: CVE-2008-4128: Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability
items.description: Cisco IOS 12.4 contains multiple cross-site forgery vulnerabilities that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via (1) a certain "show privilege" command to the /level/15/exec/- URI, and (2) a certain "alias exec" command to the /level/15/exec/-/configure/http URI. | Vendor: Cisco | Product: IOS | Action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISAs BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISAs Forensics Triage Requirements (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

items.title: NukeViet: Multiple Anti-XSS Filter Bypasses Leading to Stored XSS in News Module
items.description:
## Summary

Two filter-bypass techniques in `NukeViet\Core\Request::filterAttr()` and `NukeViet\Core\Request::unhtmlentities()` allow a low-privileged user (any account with news post permission) to store and serve arbitrary JavaScript to any visitor of the affected page.

## Affected Component

`vendor/vinades/nukeviet/Core/Request.php` class `NukeViet\Core\Request`

## Vulnerability Details

### Bypass 1 Form Feed character prefix (`\x0C`) before event handler name

The `filterAttr()` method blocks event-handler attributes using:
```php
preg_match('/^on/i', $attrSubSet[0])
```
PHP's `trim()` does **not** strip the ASCII Form Feed character (`\x0C`, U+000C). An attacker can prefix the attribute name with `\x0C` so that `\x0Conerror` does not match `/^on/`. The HTML5 browser parser tre

items.title: NukeViet: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
items.description:
## Summary

A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in NukeViet CMS versions 4.x through 4.5.08. A low-privileged authenticated user can store a JavaScript payload in their profile's display name fields. The payload executes in the browser of any visitor including administrators who clicks the **Reply** ("Answer") link on a comment posted by that user.

## Affected Component

The `{COMMENT.post_name}` template variable is interpolated without JavaScript-context escaping into an inline `onclick` handler in both comment block positions:

- `themes/default/modules/comment/comment.tpl` line 27 (top-level comments)
- `themes/default/modules/comment/comment.tpl` line 64 (nested/reply comments)

```html
onclick="nv_commment_feedback(event, {COMMENT.cid}, '{COMMENT.post_name}'

items.title: VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
items.description: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."

iocs.value: NTDS.dit access attempt via Volume Shadow Copy Service

items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Ransomware - high severity (Risk 8.5/10). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1486 (1 technique). 5 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: LockBit Ransomware Group. AI confidence: LOW (28%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 5 IOC(s) at 25.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
id: f14b858a-71a3-5a9f-a45d-ef7857371fe0
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RANSOMWARE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/13
modified: 2026/07/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_encrypt:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.locked'
            - '.encrypted'
            - '.crypted'
            - '.enc'
        EventID: 11
    selection_ransomnote:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - 'README'
            - 'HOW_TO_DECRYPT'
            - 'RECOVER_FILES'
    selection_vssdelete:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'vssadmin delete shadows'
            - 'wmic shadowcopy delete'
            - 'bcdedit /set'
    condition: selection_encrypt or selection_ransomnote or selection_vssdelete
falsepositives:
    - Encryption software (VeraCrypt, BitLocker) in use
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-13T21:14:10Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US Exploitation Attempt - RANSOMWARE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"vssadmin"; nocase; content:"delete shadows"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3570366; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service
items.description:
### Details
A number of the functions in PilotManager pass parameters directly through to the database layer, which then does not do any escaping on the parameters. For example setPilotStatus:
https://github.com/DIRACGrid/DIRAC/blob/1738e7c6d2f31d26f1364255d9d2e87b4896c922/src/DIRAC/WorkloadManagementSystem/Service/PilotManagerHandler.py#L343-L349

https://github.com/DIRACGrid/DIRAC/blob/1738e7c6d2f31d26f1364255d9d2e87b4896c922/src/DIRAC/WorkloadManagementSystem/DB/PilotAgentsDB.py#L117

This won't accept multiple statements separated by a semicolon, but a carefully crafted set of parameters containing SQL escapes would likely be able to change or return other database entries.

Further to this, the PilotManager access control is only set to "authenticated"; this allows these functions to
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] OSS-ADVISORY advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Threat Intel - high severity (Risk 8.5/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 8.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — DIRAC SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service
id: aa3dfdab-fdf4-5e29-888e-145143509e46
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: SQLI
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/13
modified: 2026/07/13
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: webserver
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        cs-uri-query|contains:
            - "' OR 1=1"
            - "UNION SELECT"
            - "'; DROP TABLE"
            - "1=1--"
            - "benchmark("
            - "sleep("
            - "0x"
        sc-status|contains: '500'
    filter_legitimate:
        cs-uri-stem|startswith: '/api/search'
    condition: selection and not filter_legitimate
falsepositives:
    - Web application firewall test traffic
    - Vulnerability scanner activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-13T21:14:10Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX DIRAC: SQL injection and lack of access control in PilotManager service Exploitation Attempt - SQLI"; flow:established,to_server; content:"UNION SELECT"; nocase; content:"--"; distance:0; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4113182; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-13, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
